trivia: The quietest member of the DC-9 lineage swapped old JT8Ds for big V2500s. It sold modestly, retired early, and found unexpected fame when NASA chose one as the bones of its X-66 research aircraft.
Only a couple of airframes remain airworthy, making this the deepest cut in the whole MD legacy section.
funFacts: NASA's X-66 sustainable flight demonstrator is a converted MD-90.
V2500 engines made it dramatically quieter than the MD-80.
